Free Pricing Cheat Sheet

Latest guides

  • Cleaning Business Client Intake Form: What to Ask First

    A cleaning business client intake form must capture property details, pets, access, priorities, lead source, and budget before you quote. Full field list inside.

  • Cleaning Business Contract Template: What It Must Cover

    A cleaning business contract template must cover scope, access, liability caps, cancellation, and your re-clean guarantee. Here's every clause, explained.

  • Cleaning Service Price List Template (Copy This Structure)

    A cleaning service price list works best as base prices by home size plus an add-on menu. Here's a copyable template and when to publish prices.

  • How Much to Charge for House Cleaning in 2026

    In 2026, solo house cleaners charge $40–75/hour and teams $50–90/hour in most US markets. Here's the flat-rate math that beats hourly quoting.

  • How Much to Charge for Mowing a Lawn in 2026

    Charge $40–$80 per cut for a typical suburban lawn in 2026, varying by lot size and market — and let route density, not the lawn, set your profit.

  • How Much to Charge to Pressure Wash a Driveway (2026)

    Charge $0.10–$0.35 per sq ft to pressure wash a driveway in 2026 — most standard driveways land at $100–$250, varying by market and condition.

All guides →

Built by an operator, not a template shop

Route Ready templates come from someone who actually ran the routes. The founder grew a route-based service company to 5,000 clients — the contracts, pricing math, and checklists in these kits are the ones that survived contact with real customers, real cancellations, and real crews. No theory, no filler.

Templates are starting points, not legal advice. Have a local attorney review contracts before use.
